page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores utilizing the Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A key characteristic of this instance is its bundled local storage, which consists of 60,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It also features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The massive local storage capacity and high-speed memory subsystem are designed to support data-intensive operations without relying on external network-attached storage.

In benchmark testing, the i7ie.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. The Passmark CPU score of 93,157.6 and Geekbench multi-core score of 21,420.0 place it in the top tier. Database operations are highly efficient, with Passmark database operations scoring 32,548.32 and Redis SET operations reaching 109.38 million operations per second. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves 548.31 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput reaches 128.24 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions support medium-model (7B) LLM prompt processing at 292.73 tokens/sec, though large-model (70B) text generation is limited to an average of 4.97 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage combined with 768 GB of DDR5 RAM provides a consolidated hardware profile that reduces the need for multi-node clustering for large datasets. This server is suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a substantial 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers top-tier benchmark results, including a Passmark CPU score of 93,157.6 and database operations scoring 32,548.32. It also achieves 109.38 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 128.24 GB/sec. While it supports medium-model LLM inference via Intel AMX instructions, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits large-model text generation. This instance is designed for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB of local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database and web workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 memory, and 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs and 320 MB L3 cache
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8 GB per core)
60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage capacity
Top-tier database operations and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving
